summary: Synthetic policy/manual PDF corpus generator: data/gen_synthetic_pdfs.py + data/synthetic_corpus/topics.yaml (10 topics) + data/test_gen_synthetic_pdfs.py. LLM content authored via agents/llm's OllamaClient (hardcoded provider="ollama", not env-driven); rendered to PDF via pymupdf/fitz (reused existing dependency, deviation from OQ-1's literal reportlab/weasyprint example list, disclosed in architecture-discovery.md and code docstring). Structured manifest.json emitted per generation run for subtask 5.1.3's OQ-2 ground-truth auto-derivation.
deviations_to_review: Uses pymupdf (fitz) for PDF *generation*, not reportlab/weasyprint literally named in OQ-1's resolution wording -- justified in architecture-discovery.md as avoiding a new dependency (pymupdf already depended on for PDF parsing).

generate_corpus()'s default LLMClient construction hardcodes create_llm_client(provider="ollama") rather than mirroring segment.py/query/server.py's zero-arg env-var-driven create_llm_client() convention -- deliberate, to satisfy the user's hard Ollama-only constraint independent of any future LLM_PROVIDER env var.

Committed topics.yaml seeds 10 topics, not the LLD's full ~30-50 -- script/design scales unchanged; smaller set chosen to keep live-Ollama self-consistency/CI runs fast. Flagged for verifier/user judgment on whether the full topic count should be filled in now or left as a documented follow-up.
notes_for_verifier: Real live-Ollama end-to-end generation was actually run this session (both via the optional pytest smoke test and a manual CLI invocation with --limit 3) and produced genuinely cross-referencing content -- recommend the verifier re-run the live smoke test independently (skippable if Ollama unreachable) rather than trusting only the mocked-LLM tests, consistent with this repo's established live-Ollama-smoke verification pattern (test_segment_live.py precedent).
